what: Restore the kept Triton MXFP4-K + FP8-V kernel from v123 as the active submission to recover from the current v089 regression
why: This branch's active submission and best_submission lag behind later kept results in tried.jsonl; restoring the stronger custom kernel is the fastest path back to the proven 18.09us regime before further tuning

what: Adaptive NSPLIT with a direct-output fast path for bs<=4,kv<=1024 and NSPLIT=4 for the other kv=1024 shapes
why: The v123 Triton kernel pays avoidable split/reduce overhead on the smallest workloads; writing stage1 directly to output for NSPLIT=1 and reducing kv=1024 splits from 8 to 4 may improve the small-shape tail without changing the large-shape path

what: Restore the last kept Triton baseline (v123) as the active submission after the reverted v136 regression
why: state/tried.jsonl shows v136 was already leaderboard-tested and catastrophically reverted, while v123 is the last kept custom kernel at 18.09us; restoring the known-good baseline is the correct recovery before further tuning

what_failed: Coordinator requested session reset after adapting workflow to new rate limits.
